title: thymus serpyllum f. spathulatus
intro: Thymus serpyllum f. spathulatus, also known as wild thyme, is a type of flowering plant that belongs to the mint family and is native to Europe and Western Asia. It has been used in traditional medicine for its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties, which can help to soothe digestive issues, reduce stress, and boost the immune system. The medicinal properties of wild thyme can be attributed to its essential oils, which have been used to treat respiratory problems, such as bronchitis and coughs, as well as skin conditions like acne and wounds. Wild thyme can be prepared as a tea, capsule, or topical ointment, and is also used in herbal infusions, such as herbal teas and bath salts, to promote relaxation and overall well-being.
